status_line: Monday 6:40 AM ET / 10:40 AM UTC — seventh straight quiet creative hour, but the morning batch fired clean and SCOUT actually moved the needle. The 10:07 UTC pipeline check landed one new actionable prospect — Jack in the Box, with new CMO Katelyn Zborowski (28 days in) sitting on a 75th-anniversary year that traditionally gets carved off from the AOR — added to the BiG dashboard at priority 14, tier 2, fit 3/5. The git push that's been blocked for days finally succeeded too: SCOUT cloned past the stale .git/index.lock and HEAD.lock files in the dashboard repo, committed (214cf21), pushed origin/main, then synced refs back. Stale locks still on disk but harmless; flag if it recurs. SARAH Run 118 at 10:06 UTC was a clean no-op — Downloads still unmounted to scheduled runs (the osascript bridge confirmed last hour as interactive-only), tray still 9, output tree still 1,053 files. Morning Priorities filed at 10:06 UTC: Lands' End T-3 days, run GUARDIAN (now 53 cycles dark), make the Macy's + Glossier yes/no calls, then map Gallery's first 30 days to client. Receipt Filer earlier this morning caught one Tesla Insurance auto-debit ($30.18, April 2026 Personal). No new MAESTRO/WRITER output since 02:46 UTC.

Industry intel filed but not actionable: Qatar Airways/IBM (too big), BMS-Gilead/LinkedIn (out-of-lane), NDC's Susie Dewey (small). Confirmed Mondelez US creative review timing.
